name: DrugCentral
description: A drug information resource providing drug-disease treatment relationships (indications, off-label uses) and pharmacologic class annotations. Maintained by the University of New Mexico.
access_mode: PostgreSQL database loaded from a gzipped SQL dump

instructions.python_access: Connect via psycopg2. The parser defaults to host=localhost, port=5432, dbname=drugcentral. Override by adding a pg_config block in config/databases.yaml under drugcentral.args. Do NOT use user_env/password_env keys unless the env vars are actually set — unset _env keys resolve to None and break the psycopg2 connection.
inspect: psql drugcentral -c "SELECT relationship_name, count(*) FROM omop_relationship GROUP BY 1;"
psql drugcentral -c "SELECT type, count(*) FROM pharma_class GROUP BY 1;"
gotchas: The _resolve_env_vars function in main.py converts user_env/password_env to user: None / password: None when env vars are unset. psycopg2 treats None as empty string, which breaks authentication. Only add _env keys to databases.yaml if the env vars are guaranteed to be set.
The parser sets search_path=public via the psycopg2 options parameter. If tables are not found, verify the search_path is correct.

omop_relationship.description: Drug-disease links. Use relationship_name IN ('indication', 'off-label use')

columns.concept_id: integer, OMOP concept ID for disease

pharma_class.description: Pharmacologic class definitions AND struct-to-class mapping in one table. No separate junction table exists.

columns.type: varchar(20), class type (e.g. 'Mechanism of Action', 'Physiologic Effect', 'Chemical/Ingredient', 'Chemical Structure')
columns.name: varchar(1000), class name

identifier.description: Maps struct_id to external database IDs. Use id_type = 'DRUGBANK_ID' to get DrugBank IDs.
